channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Rose & Camellia Collection ‘Online Battle Mode’ update launches in June
item.content:encoded:
• Switch

Slap-fight against players across the globe.

Publisher PLAYISM and developer Nigoro have will release a free update for slap battle action game Rose & Camellia Collection in June that adds an online battle mode, the companies announced.

Rose & Camellia Collection is available now for Switch.

Watch a new trailer below.

“Online Battle Mode” Update Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/rose-camellia-collection-online-battle-mode-update-launches-in-june
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Idol Manager: Virtual Venture announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Pop star management simulation sequel due out in 2027.

Publisher PLAYISM and developer Glitch Pitch have announced Idol Manager: Virtual Venture, a sequel to the pop star management simulation game Idol Manager. It will launch in Early Access for PC via Steam in 2027 with English, Japanese, and Simplified Chinese language options, with additional languages to be determined.

Here is an overview of the game, via PLAYISM:

About

Climb to the top of the entertainment industry, no matter what it takes…

The idol agency management simulation Idol Manager, critically acclaimed for its exploration of the harsh realities of the pop idol industry, will finally become a series with this new entry, Idol Manager: Virtual Venture.

This time, your stars are virtual streamers! As a manager at a newly established streaming corporation, players will strive to take their streamers to the top of the virtual streaming industry.

With the same difficult management simulation as the last game, Virtual Venture introduces many new features specific to virtual streaming.

In the previous game, your agency only accepted female idols, but in Virtual Venture, male streamers are more than welcome! Help them customize their avatars, streaming assets, background music, and more to produce new and original virtual streamers for your agency.

Just like in Idol Manager, you can chat with your streamers to build up trust, but friendships aren’t the only type of relationship in your agency. Look out for internal conflicts within your streamer groups and watch as both the pretty and ugly sides of them emerge.

Will you lead your streamers to a bright, promising future, or a dark, grueling one? It’s up to you to bridge the gap between the virtual and the real and survive in this demanding industry. Will you be the producer of the world’s best virtual streamer?

Key Features

  • A fusion between management sim and visual novel with plenty of dark humor.
  • Hold auditions to scout streamers, from complete newbies to famous experienced indies (both male and female).
  • Create avatars, hand-pick stream assets, moderate chats, and select suitable music for your streamers for the best setup.
  • React to viewers in the chat with an interactive streaming system.
  • Expand your business and customize your agency to your liking.
  • Streamer dorm rooms that change depending on their characteristics.
  • Deepen your relationships with your streamers and observe relationships between the streamers as well, such as friendships, rivalries, romances, and internal conflicts.
  • Random events that affect your streamers’ careers, including spicy scandals.
  • Manage your streamers’ social media, decide what posts they like or reply to, and report bad actors trying to ruin your fun.
  • Chat with your streamers to get their opinion on you and gossip about relationships within the agency.
  • Both a full-fledged story mode and a management-focused freeplay mode.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/idol-manager-virtual-venture-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Deckbuilding autobattler Samurai Bringer: Rampage announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
Publisher PLAYISM and developer Alphawing have announced Samurai Bringer: Rampage, a deckbuilding autobattler entry in the Bringer series. It will launch for PC via Steam in 2026 with English, Japanese, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese language support.

Here is an overview of the game, via PLAYISM:

About

Alphawing, the developers of Samurai Bringer and Metal Bringer, will be releasing a new entry in the Bringer series—the deckbuilding autobattler Samurai Bringer: Rampage!

Samurai Bringer, the first game in the series, is a roguelite action game where you slay samurai and yokai alike in stages that change every run. It has gained over 900 reviews on Steam with a Very Positive rating.

Samurai Bringer: Rampage preserves the comical voxel samurai action of Samurai Bringer, but mixes things up with a brand new game system as a deckbuilding autobattler.

Players will select their samurai generals and build formations based on their samurai’s weapons and abilities. Fight your way through the war-ridden Sengoku period and come out on top to unite the land.

Form Your Army and Unite the Land

Arrange your samurai and strengthen your army to prepare for battle! Match weapon types and abilities to trigger synergies that enhance your formation. Adopt different strategies to add traits to your formation, focus on rushing the enemy early, or build up your strength over time. Survey the enemy’s formation and strategy, then rearrange your own forces to conquer the land!

Flashy Autobattling Fights

Battles are fought automatically against formations created by players from around the world and saved on the server. Enjoy chaotic battles with over-the-top, flashy attacks!

Choose Your General

Each General offers different starting samurai and strategies. Pick your favorite historical general, choose one whose strategy fits the formation you want to build, or even start from your preferred region. You are in command!

Game Modes

  • Hado (Supremacy) – Fight against formations made by players worldwide, raise your military merits, and surpass the gods of war.
  • Gido (Simulation) – Relaxing mock battles. Find the key to victory that works for you.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/deckbuilding-autobattler-samurai-bringer-rampage-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ‘Devilishly cheeky adventure game’ DevilConnection coming to Switch in 2026
item.content:encoded:
• Switch

Currently available for PC.

PLAYISM will release a Switch version of ChaoGames-developed “devilishly cheeky adventure game” DevilConnection in 2026, the publisher announced. It will support English, Japanese, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese language options.

DevilConnection first launched for PC via Steam on October 30, 2025.

Here is an overview of the game, via PLAYISM:

About

DevilConnection, where you join forces with the bratty devil Devilun to forcefully summon colorful characters and get emotional reactions out of them, has gotten great critical acclaim with over 1,600 reviews and an Overwhelmingly Positive rating on Steam. And now, the devilishly cheeky adventure game will be coming to the Nintendo Switch in 2026!

The Nintendo Switch will add English and Chinese (Traditional and Simplified) languages and optimized controller support, providing a fresh gameplay experience where you can’t help but want to teach that naughty yet cute little Devilun a lesson.

Story

In the magical world of Magirisia…

You, a summoner, have summoned a devil. You give him your own name to make a contract, but the devil does not give his name.

You creatively name him Devilun, who, in order to regain his true form, summon and then terrorize the people of Magirisia to drain their mana!

System

Summon colorful characters with Devilun to extract their “mood auras” and drain their mana.

You’ll have to choose the right options to heighten your victim’s emotions. Don’t worry, you don’t have to choose blindly! Use Devilun’s special ability “Evil Eye Search” to peek inside your opponent’s mind. By reading their thoughts, you will naturally find the correct answer, or you may even catch a glimpse of something surprising…

A defining feature of this game is the sheer number of endings. A wrong choice can result in an immediate bad ending… But you can use the magic of “saving” and “loading” to explore all the possibilities and discover the true ending!

You want to see all the endings…so maybe you want to pick the obviously wrong choice sometimes. This is a game of doing all sorts of dastardly deeds with a devil, after all.

Key Features

  • More than 30 summonable characters.
  • Fully animated characters with lively expressions for maximum immersion.
  • Tap the characters’ bodies and enjoy their different reactions!
  • Fantastical, sometimes dark and gothic soundtrack perfect for this magical world.
  • The sort of naughtiness and expression of the author’s interests you can only find in an indie game.

Watch a new trailer below.

Switch Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/devilishly-cheeky-adventure-game-devilconnection-coming-to-switch-in-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Comedic text-based adventure game There’s Something Wrong with the Hero’s Choices announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
Publisher PLAYISM and developer minatonezumi have announced comedic text-based adventure game There’s Something Wrong with the Hero’s Choices. It will launch for PC via Steam in 2026 with English, Japanese, and Korean language support.

Here is an overview of the game, via PLAYISM:

About

There’s Something Wrong with the Hero’s Choices, the winner of the PLAYISM award in the Niconico Indies Game Fest 2020 with a free game version released on May 2, 2025, is a text-based adventure game featuring a party of heroes on a journey to slay the Dark Lord, but something about the hero’s dialogue choices is off… And now, it’s coming to Steam in 2026!

The Steam version is enhanced from the free version, with new episodes and additional English and Korean language support. Journey in the world of There’s Something Wrong with the Hero’s Choices in English for the very first time!

With over 2,000 event illustrations and over 30 endings, you’ll have to explore your (weird) options to reach the best ending!

Story

In a faraway kingdom under a crimson moon…

A party of heroes is ordered by the king to slay the Dark Lord.

Faced with treacherous terrain and ferocious lackeys of the Dark Lord, the hero is forced to make decisions at every turn to ensure a safe journey. Can the party reach the Dark Lord and complete their mission?

The Party

The healer, Shayla.

The mage, Silvan.

And the hero, from a line of legendary heroes…

The physical attacker, John Doe.

John Doe…? Really…?

Key Features

  • A text-based adventure with head-scratching dialogue choices.
  • Unexpected twists and turns based on your choices, and sometimes abrupt endings…
  • Over 2,000 event illustrations and over 30 endings.
  • Save anywhere, anytime. Reach the best ending through trial and error!
  • Extra episodes and character sheets for an enhanced experience from the award-winning free version.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/comedic-text-based-adventure-game-theres-something-wrong-with-the-heros-choices-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 2D exploration horror adventure game remake Midnight Train: New Moon announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
Publisher PLAYISM and developer LydiaBluebell have announced 2D exploration horror adventure game remake Midnight Train: New Moon. It will launch for PC via Steam in 2027 with English, Spanish, Japanese, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese language support.

Here is an overview of the game, via PLAYISM:

About

Midnight Train: New Moon is a 2D exploration-based horror adventure game developed by Spain-based indie game studio LydiaBluebell. It is a remake of the free RPG Maker game Midnight Train, originally released in 2020. Although the original hails from Spain, it belongs to the lineage of traditional horror RPGs nurtured within Japan’s free game culture, and garnered high praise, particularly among free game fans outside Japan.

Midnight Train: New Moon retains the story-driven experience of uncovering the truth through exploration and puzzle-solving, while featuring completely revamped graphics, visual effects, and game balance. It has been rebuilt so that both newcomers and those who have played the original can enjoy it with a fresh perspective.

Guided by a train, Luna arrives at a strange building. Through exploration and puzzle-solving, she aims to escape. Inexplicable events, crises that arrive without warning, and the truth revealed in fragments—the full picture of the story gradually becomes clear.

Story

Luna Wyndell is a girl traveling by train in search of a certain place. Before she knows it, all the passengers inside the train have vanished without a trace, and the train has stopped in a strange void.

Together with Neil, an aspiring detective also left behind on the train, she steps into a building in the void. There, the two discover a strange pocket watch, along with a note:

“Find the next train. It departs in three hours. If you fail, you’ll be trapped here forever.”

Key Features

  • A story that spans four chapters taking place on the train and inside the mysterious building.
  • A game system where you gather clues through exploration and solve mysteries.
  • Multiple endings that branch based on the player’s choices.
  • Maps with different designs, and various characters that appear in each chapter.
  • An original soundtrack that deepens immersion in the world.
  • Sprite animations that bring scenes to life.
  • Partial voice acting that enhances the characters.

Remake Elements

  • Completely revamped graphics.
  • Redesigned puzzles and trials with adjusted difficulty.
  • New events and visual effects.
  • Quality-of-life improvements.

Characters

  • Luna Wyndell – A girl traveling in search of a certain place. Left behind on the train, she meets Neil and, aiming to escape from the strange void, steps into the building that stands beside the train. Unable to speak, she uses a notebook to communicate with others.
  • Neil Lawton – An aspiring detective travelling in pursuit of that dream. Left behind on the train like Luna, he ends up going together with her. Though kind-hearted, he can also be timid and unreliable at times. However, when it comes to deduction, he displays keen observational skills and unravels numerous mysteries.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/2d-exploration-horror-adventure-game-remake-midnight-train-new-moon-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: No Case Should Remain Unsolved coming to PS5 this summer
item.content:encoded:
• PS5

Currently available for Switch and PC.

PLAYISM will release Somi-developed mystery adventure game No Case Should Remain Unsolved for PlayStation 5 this summer, the companies announced.

No Case Should Remain Unsolved first launched for PC via Steam on January 17, 2024, followed by Switch on September 19, 2024.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

“I remember how surprised I was when I first saw this case. Everyone involved was lying, for one reason or another.”

No Case Should Remain Unsolved is a detective game where players uncover and piece together memory fragments to solve a long-forgotten case. Follow former police detective Jeon Gyeong as she acquires clues and testimony relating to the case of a missing girl.

Story

February 5, 2012. A little girl named Seowon is reported missing from a playground. Police launch an investigation and question witnesses and suspects, but Seowon’s case is never solved.

Twelve years after Senior Inspector Jeon Gyeong’s retirement, she is visited by a young police officer: a woman who pleads with her to reexamine Seowon’s case.

But with each uncovered memory, only one thing becomes clear: everyone in Seowon’s vicinity was lying.

Key Features

  • Experience an imaginative reasoning system where players connect memories like pieces of a jigsaw puzzle.
  • Uncover passcodes, keys, 54 conversations, and two hidden truths.
  • Over the course of a two to three-hour playthrough, players will learn why Seowon’s disappearance is the only case Jeon Gyeong has left unsolved.
  • Also featuring an original soundtrack by Seongyi Yi, composer of Legal Dungeon and The Wake!

Watch a new trailer below.

PlayStation 5 Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/no-case-should-remain-unsolved-coming-to-ps5-this-summer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
